full_text:
Final results

Cell cycle project ( before AACRovc)

Cell composition

Cell composition vs markers

Proliferation and arrest scores

How did I calculate the scores

Proliferation score (-1:1) -> (pcna+ki67)/2
Arrest score (-1,:1) -> (0.4p21+0.6p27)/2

“Gated” individually for each image to establish thresholds:

Cell composition all samples

Proportion of states within compartments

Statistical tests

Proportion of proliferation/MPI within compartments

Long vs Short PFS

Not significant: CD4 T cells_Non_proliferative (p-value: 0.09259546498022503, Fold Change: 1.8513954475547862) 
Not significant: Cancer_Proliferative (p-value: 0.23461640506486223, Fold Change: 1.650387394060172)

CD8 T cells_Arrested (p-value: 0.03684581898970713, Fold Change: 0.2971563372612724)

Long vs Short PFS (In Post samples)

Not significant: CD4 T cells_Proliferative (p-value: 0.6063995131869717, Fold Change: 1.6049336125163123) 
Not significant: CD8 T cells_Proliferative (p-value: 0.5646751092778556, Fold Change: 1.504831297736487) 
Not significant: Cancer_Proliferative (p-value: 0.361014089885884, Fold Change: 1.6496961843904123) 
Not significant: Stromal_Proliferative (p-value: 0.3755466794450988, Fold Change: 1.8111055380349956)

No results when comparing Pre vs Post in pairs

Long vs Short PFS (In Pre samples)

Not significant: CD4 T cells_Non_proliferative (p-value: 0.14828141983358958, Fold Change: 2.1216333172343025) Not significant: Cancer_Proliferative (p-value: 0.4726939869876137, Fold Change: 1.7050121017731643)

Long vs Short PFS

Left cluster had higher proportions of myeloid and cancer cells

Right had higher percentage of stromal and cd4 t cells

Spatial analysis

Spatial distances

Arrested cells seem to be close to each other in hubs -> 
I think it is artificial, due to my scoring?
Too many common patterns

DIVIDE THEM IN LONG AND POST

Spatial coocurrence

Arrested cells seem to be close to each other in hubs -> 
I think it is artificial, due to my scoring?
Too many common patterns

Proliferative myeloids are closer to proliferative cell types (specially to other myeloids)

All cell types seem to be differentially closer to arrested cancer cells than to non-proliferative and proliferative cancer cells

Phenotype: CD4 T cells, NACT status: Pre, Marker: PCNA_y, Fold Change SHORT VS LONG: 1.673 Phenotype: CD4 T cells, NACT status: Pre, Marker: CyclinD1_y, Fold Change SHORT VS LONG: 1.716 Phenotype: CD8 T cells, NACT status: Pre, Marker: PCNA_y, Fold Change SHORT VS LONG: 1.403 Phenotype: Cancer, NACT status: Post, Marker: PCNA_y, Fold Change LONG VS SHORT: 1.406 Phenotype: Cancer, NACT status: Post, Marker: CyclinD1_y, Fold Change LONG VS SHORT: 1.653 Phenotype: Cancer, NACT status: Pre, Marker: Ki67_y, Fold Change SHORT VS LONG: 2.880 Phenotype: Cancer, NACT status: Pre, Marker: PCNA_y, Fold Change SHORT VS LONG: 1.842 Phenotype: Myeloid, NACT status: Pre, Marker: PCNA_y, Fold Change SHORT VS LONG: 1.499
